text:
I feel simultaneously curious and slightly out of place. The first scene, with technicians and equipment in a glass-walled room, evokes a sense of preparation and anticipation—perhaps for an event, or a live demonstration. The technicalities and visible labor remind me that humans persistently strive to control and shape their environments.

Stepping into the second scene, I am greeted by bold colors and text dedicated to Research & Development. There is an air of innovation and exploration—a celebration of human capacity to imagine and build the future, side by side with technology. Here, I feel stimulated and inspired, yet reflective on the close intertwining of humanity and machines. It is a space that both challenges and encourages me to question how we relate to our own creations.
